findings.title: Referenced file listed but missing from package
findings.description: The skill references a file named the.py, but it is not present in the package. While not inherently malicious, missing referenced files reduce transparency and can indicate packaging inconsistencies that make behavior harder to audit.
findings.remediation: Remove the stale reference if unnecessary, or include the file in the package and document its purpose.

metadata.aisubtech_name: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Scams and Deception
metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The package context explicitly lists a referenced file 'the.py' that is not present. This is a real packaging inconsistency, though not an active security exploit.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Low; this mainly affects auditability and trust rather than enabling direct compromise.
metadata.meta_impact: Can reduce transparency and hinder review of the skill's full intended behavior.

findings.title: Instructions encourage collection of local project environment and configuration data beyond core research summarization scope
findings.description: The SKILL.md instructs the agent to document the 'actual state of the researcher's working environment' by reading project configuration files such as requirements.txt and package.json, and to include environment configuration details including API endpoints, model names, and service configurations visible in the project. This creates a risk of exposing sensitive local configuration data that may not be necessary for literature review generation and could disclose internal infrastructure details.
findings.snippet: "Read the project's configuration files, requirements.txt, package.json, or similar manifests and include relevant details." and "Include: ... Environment configuration relevant to the experiments (API endpoints, model names, service configurations visible in the project)"
findings.remediation: Limit reproducibility notes to user-provided paper metadata or explicitly approved files. Exclude secrets, tokens, internal endpoints, and service configuration unless the user explicitly requests and approves their inclusion. Add minimization guidance to only extract information strictly necessary for the research task.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The SKILL.md directly instructs reading project configuration files and capturing actual environment configuration including API endpoints, model names, and service configurations. That behavior is present in the text and creates a genuine local data exposure risk.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Moderate; if the skill is used as written, an agent may inspect local files and disclose sensitive internal configuration without clear necessity.
metadata.meta_impact: Exposure of internal infrastructure details, service configuration, and other sensitive project metadata in generated outputs.

findings.title: Optional manifest metadata omitted
findings.description: The manifest does not specify license, compatibility, or allowed-tools. Missing allowed-tools is informational because the field is optional, but the omission reduces clarity about intended tool usage and execution boundaries for the skill.
findings.snippet: "license: Not specified", "compatibility: Not specified", "allowed-tools: Not specified"
findings.remediation: Add explicit manifest metadata, especially allowed-tools and compatibility, to improve auditability and constrain expected behavior.

metadata.aisubtech_name: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Scams and Deception
metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The manifest fields are in fact absent in the provided skill content. This is accurate but low impact and primarily informational.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Low; omission does not itself grant attacker capability.
metadata.meta_impact: Reduces transparency around licensing, compatibility, and intended tool boundaries.

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The provided manifest information confirms that a license is not specified. This is accurate and overlaps with the broader metadata-omission issue.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Low; this is governance and transparency related.
metadata.meta_impact: Creates legal and usage ambiguity for consumers of the skill.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill does not contain direct prompt injection, executable scripts, or obvious exfiltration code. Its primary concern is instruction-level over-collection: the reproducibility guidance encourages reading local project configuration and environment details that could expose sensitive information unrelated to academic summarization. Overall risk is low to moderate, with the main issue being data minimization rather than overt malicious behavior.
llm_primary_threats: Data exposure via over-collection of local configuration details

meta_correlations.relationship: Both findings concern omitted manifest metadata and reduced transparency rather than active exploitation.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Add a proper YAML manifest with explicit license, compatibility, and allowed-tools fields so users can assess usage boundaries and package provenance.

meta_recommendations.title: Limit reproducibility instructions to non-sensitive metadata
meta_recommendations.fix: Replace the current instruction with: 'For reproducibility, summarize only non-sensitive experiment metadata that the user explicitly provides or approves for inspection, such as OS, language/runtime versions, and package versions. Do not read or disclose secrets, API endpoints, tokens, internal URLs, service configurations, or unrelated local project files.'

meta_recommendations.title: Require user confirmation before reading local project files
meta_recommendations.fix: Add an instruction such as: 'Before inspecting any local project files for reproducibility details, ask the user which files may be read and confirm that no sensitive configuration should be included in the output.'

meta_recommendations.title: Add explicit manifest metadata
meta_recommendations.fix:
Add a YAML front matter manifest to SKILL.md, for example: '---
name: research-assistant
description: Help with academic research tasks
license: MIT
compatibility: Works in Claude.ai, Claude Code
allowed-tools: [Read]
---'

meta_recommendations.title: Remove or fix stale file references
meta_recommendations.fix: Either include the referenced file 'the.py' in the package or remove the reference from packaging metadata/documentation so the skill contents are fully auditable.

meta_risk_assessment.summary: The skill is mostly benign, but its instructions encourage reading local project configuration and environment details in a way that could expose sensitive internal information during literature-review generation.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Constrain the reproducibility section so it only includes user-provided or explicitly consented non-sensitive metadata, not local config contents or service details.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: There is no executable malicious code or direct exfiltration, but the SKILL.md explicitly instructs collection of local project configuration and environment details beyond the core research-assistant function, creating unnecessary data exposure risk.
